title: Vance’s Defense of Iran Deal Rests on Vague and Misleading Claims
summary: The vice president said the United States had leverage to dictate the outcome of the next round of negotiations. But he claimed incorrectly that Iran got no new benefit from the lifting of oil sanctions.

title: Iran and the U.S. Have an Understanding. Will It Lead to a Deal?
summary: Europe and the larger world will be watching carefully to see if talks produce a lasting agreement on Iran’s nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Vance Issues a Blunt Warning to Israel
summary: Also, Ukraine launches a major assault on Moscow. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

title: Firm Tied to Trump Donor Got No-Bid Contract to Clean Reflecting Pool
summary: A White House spokeswoman said the president was not involved in selecting Greenwater Services, the business owned by a trust led by John J. Cafaro.

title: Trump Redirects Millions From Secret Service Amid White House Construction
summary: The White House insists that private donations are paying for a new ballroom, but the administration is using taxpayer money for a series of security upgrades.

title: Trump’s Intelligence Pick Renews Debate: Is the Director of National Intelligence Needed?
summary: The director of national intelligence’s office was created in the aftermath of the Sept. 11 attacks. Now some lawmakers want to shrink it.

title: NYC Erupts in Celebration Over Knicks Championship Win
summary: New York City found a reason to party on Thursday, cheering, crying and climbing around Broadway to celebrate the Knicks’ N.B.A. championship.

title: See the Knicks Championship Parade Unfold
summary: Fans gathered in Manhattan on Thursday to cheer on the N.B.A. champions at a parade that has been 53 years in the making.

title: Rama Duwaji Had the Winning Look at the Knicks Parade
summary: The first lady and the mayor showed up for the celebration in style.

title: Burnham Wins Makerfield By-Election, Clearing Path to Challenge Starmer as UK Prime Minister
summary: Andy Burnham, Labour’s most popular politician, beat the populist right-wing Reform U.K. party in the Makerfield by-election. The decisive victory will energize his bid to become prime minister.

title: Who Is Andy Burnham, the Man Who Could Be Britain’s Next Prime Minister?
summary: Charismatic, northern and exuding a relaxed optimism, Mr. Burnham is a contrast to Keir Starmer. His allies hope he could mend Labour’s relationship with voters.

title: Takeaways From ‘Regime Change,’ Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan’s Book on Trump’s White House
summary: The book by Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan of The New York Times, “Regime Change,” reveals a host of details and surprising exchanges as President Trump pushed to drastically expand his power.

title: Ukraine Strikes Moscow Refinery in Large-Scale Drone Attack
summary: The attack, which shut down the capital’s airports for several hours, was part of an escalating campaign to bring the conflict home to Russians.

title: A Twist in Ukraine’s Drone Campaign Is ‘Really Hurting the Russians’
summary: Midrange attacks, using upgraded drones that Ukraine produces in huge numbers, are causing fuel shortages and complicating troop rotations.

title: Scores Fall Ill at Air Force Base After Hegseth Makes Flu Vaccine Optional
summary: The defense secretary described the vaccine requirement, which he lifted in April, as an “absurd, overreaching” mandate.

title: Colorado Governor Overruled His Own Clemency Board to Release Tina Peters
summary: Two members of Gov. Jared Polis’s board that reviews commutation requests came forward to say the secretive panel had twice rejected the release of Tina Peters, convicted of election tampering.

title: The Major Oak, Ancient Tree of Robin Hood Legend, Has Died
summary: The Major Oak in the Sherwood Forest was between 800 and 1,200 years old. It succumbed to a combination of over-tourism, climate change and misguided efforts to save it.

title: In Ancient Pits Near Stonehenge, Scientists See Hints of Solstice Ritual
summary: British archaeologists may have found the remains of a site where people celebrated the solstice thousands of years ago, a few miles from the famed stone circle.

title: Gary Shteyngart, a Russian-Born Author, Tours Thomas Jefferson’s Home With His Son
summary: I emigrated from the Soviet Union decades ago, and recently toured Thomas Jefferson’s home with my American-born history-buff son.

title: Barack Obama Presidential Center Opens in Chicago
summary: Thursday’s opening drew four former presidents, as well as Bruce Springsteen, Bono and Jennifer Hudson. Chicago has seen years of planning and legal fights.

title: Here’s What Barack and Michelle Obama Said at His Presidential Center Opening
summary: The Obamas each took to the microphone, delivering speeches before a crowd of dignitaries, elected officials and Democratic supporters.

title: Stephen Colbert Nods to Obama’s Tan Suit at Presidential Center Opening
summary: Celebrity guests paid winking homage to President Obama’s notorious tan suit.

title: During Trump’s Immigration Crackdown, Minnesota Protester Remained Shackled in Hospital for Days
summary: The story of one progressive activist arrested in Minnesota in January shows what critics say is the aggressive nature of the Trump administration’s response to those who have protested its immigration crackdown.

title: ICE Spent $700 Million on 7 Warehouses. Now It Wants to Get Rid of Them.
summary: Immigration and Customs Enforcement plans to sell or give away most of the 11 warehouses it bought to detain migrants, reversing course on a signature initiative.

title: Central Park Rides Halted After Fatal Fall From Horse-Drawn Carriage
summary: An 18-year-old was ejected after a carriage horse bolted on Wednesday as the driver took a photograph. It was unclear how long the voluntary shutdown might last.

title: James Talarico Attacks Ken Paxton Over Child Abuse Case
summary: The case, which resulted in a short jail sentence despite years of alleged abuse, has drawn outcry in Texas. So have the attempts to politicize it.

title: Absent Congressman to Return to House in 12 Days, Aide Says
summary: Thomas Kean Jr. has missed three months of votes in Washington and has not been on the campaign trail in his New Jersey district because of an unexplained health condition.

title: Democratic Socialist Defeats Centrist in the D.C. Mayoral Primary
summary: President Trump’s grip on the capital loomed over the race, and he threatened to tighten it if Councilwoman Janeese Lewis George is elected. She almost certainly will be.

title: What Young Men Thought of Trump’s U.F.C. Fight
summary: The spectacle seemed targeted to the young male voters drifting away from the president. Some were skeptical.

title: Mariska Hargitay on ‘Every Brilliant Thing’ and Finding Inspiration From Jalen Brunson
summary: The “Law & Order: SVU” actress “was really scared” about starring in “Every Brilliant Thing.” Who does she look to for inspiration? The Knicks, of course.

title: JD Vance on the Morality of the Trump Administration
summary: I asked the vice president what is Christian about this White House.

title: Trump and Netanyahu Have Really Stepped in It Now
summary: America and Israel have snatched defeat from victory.

title: Jean Houston, ‘Midwife of Souls’ Who Advised Hillary Clinton, Dies at 89
summary: The author of books like “The Possible Human,” she held workshops that drew on mythology, psychology and the experiential ethos of Esalen. But she refused to be called a guru.
